div.head: The Acting Secretary of State to the Ambassador in China (Hurley)
div.p: 889. Personal for the Ambassador. I have seen a copy of your telegram of June 6 to Secretary Morgenthau in reply to his message giving you background information on the supply of gold to China. I feel that you should know that State, Treasury and other interested agencies reached a decision on this matter only after full consideration of all factors involved and on the basis of all available information. If there is a shortcoming it arises out of the oversight of the Department in not sending to you accounts of the progress of the conversations held.
